message:
feat(bubbletea): Improve command, message, and program modules

Enhance the BubbleTea Rust port with improved command handling,
message passing, and program lifecycle management.

- Update command.rs with enhanced execution logic
- Improve message.rs type definitions
- Enhance program.rs runtime handling
- Sync beads issue tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-212m.4.2","title":"Huh: verify Catppuccin theme parity","description":"Verify Catppuccin theme parity (colors and styles) against Go Huh.

Scope:
- Compare Rust Catppuccin theme definitions to Go legacy_huh (Latte/Frappe/Macchiato/Mocha).
- Ensure theme slots map to identical RGB/ANSI values and styling (bold/italic).
- Add unit tests for theme color slots and integrate conformance fixtures if needed.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- theme_catppuccin fixture passes with exact Go output.
- Unit tests validate color slot values and style flags for Catppuccin themes.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md reflects verified Catppuccin parity.","notes":"Files: crates/huh/src/lib.rs theme definitions; legacy_huh for reference; tests/conformance/crates/huh/mod.rs fixture theme_catppuccin.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:25.118504364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T17:10:16.163497191Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T17:10:16.163244896Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:25.118504364Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.4.3","title":"Huh: fix KeyMap file_picker initializer compile error","description":"Benchmark E2E compile currently fails because KeyMap initializer does not include file_picker in at least one cfg path. This blocks benchmark_e2e::compilation_tests::test_benchmarks_compile during cargo test -p charmed_conformance.

Scope:
- Find all KeyMap initializers and feature-gated cfgs in crates/huh/src/lib.rs (and any tests/benches).
- Ensure file_picker: FilePickerKeyMap::default() is set everywhere a KeyMap is constructed.
- Add regression coverage (unit + compile in bench path) and structured logs.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- cargo check -p huh --all-targets passes (no missing field errors).
- cargo test -p charmed_conformance -- --nocapture passes benchmark_e2e::compilation_tests::test_benchmarks_compile.
- New unit test (e.g., test_keymap_includes_file_picker) asserts default KeyMap includes file_picker bindings.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated with latest run details.","notes":"Checked crates/huh/src/lib.rs: KeyMap::new includes file_picker; no other KeyMap struct literals present. `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` and benchmark_e2e::compilation_tests::test_benchmarks_compile now pass (CARGO_HOME=target/mistyotter_cargo_home6 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/mistyotter_benchfix), so original missing-field error is no longer reproducible. Likely fixed by recent edits; closing as resolved.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:21:51.845194607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T02:05:30.273493830Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T02:05:30.273288794Z","close_reason":"No longer reproducible; KeyMap::new includes file_picker and bench compile passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:21:51.845194607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.5","title":"Bubbletea parity gaps","description":"Close Bubbletea parity gaps: custom I/O event injection and mouse drag support evaluation.

Scope:
- Implement custom I/O event injection path for non-standard inputs (per program.rs note).
- Evaluate mouse drag support vs Go and decide implementation plan.
- Add unit + conformance tests to ensure expected message flow and mouse events.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Custom I/O injection works and is covered by tests/fixtures.
- Mouse drag support evaluated with documented decision (implemented or explicitly excluded) and FEATURE_PARITY.md updated.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs, crates/bubbletea/src/input.rs (if present), tests/conformance/crates/bubbletea/mod.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:32.471902669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:27:24.610035289Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.5","depends_on_id":"bd-212m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:32.471902669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-212m.5.1","title":"Bubbletea: implement custom I/O event injection","description":"Implement custom I/O event injection path in Bubbletea to match Go behavior.

Scope:
- Use program.rs note at line ~432: custom I/O assumes events injected via other means.
- Design a public API for injecting input events/messages when Program is configured with custom input/output streams.
- Ensure injection integrates with the event loop and preserves ordering with crossterm events.
- Add unit tests for injected events and conformance fixtures if needed.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- New API allows injecting events/messages without crossterm (custom I/O mode).
- Unit tests cover injection ordering, shutdown, and error handling.
- Conformance tests updated to include a custom I/O injection fixture (if Go supports it).
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to remove Bubbletea custom I/O gap.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (event loop), crates/bubbletea/src/cmd.rs (if relevant), tests/conformance/crates/bubbletea/mod.rs. Maintain async + sync parity.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:39.053570122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:27:32.293668078Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:39.053570122Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Maintain async + sync parity.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:39.053570122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:48:19.619731277Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:39.053570122Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.5.2","title":"Bubbletea: evaluate mouse drag support","description":"Evaluate and optionally implement mouse drag support to match Go Bubbletea.

Scope:
- Compare Rust mouse event parsing with Go (drag/selection events).
- Identify gaps in crossterm mouse event support and how Bubbletea maps them to messages.
- Decide: implement drag event synthesis or document limitation.
- Add tests or documentation updates accordingly.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Decision documented: implement drag events or explicitly exclude with rationale.
- If implemented: unit tests + conformance fixtures for drag sequences.
- README limitations updated if drag remains limited.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/input.rs (mouse parsing), crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (event loop), README.md limitations section.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:46.941228385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T17:17:16.388008775Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T17:17:16.387905270Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:46.941228385Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.6","title":"Bubbles parity audit & fixture coverage","description":"Audit Bubbles component parity and fixture coverage. Although current conformance passes, we need to ensure coverage is comprehensive and that any skip stubs are removed or replaced with real tests.

Scope:
- Component-by-component parity audits (stopwatch, timer, list, table, paginator, help, viewport, cursor, keybinding, filepicker, textinput).
- Expand fixtures and unit tests to cover edge cases and Go behavior.
- Remove dead SKIPPED branches once fixtures are comprehensive.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Each component audit produces either (a) confirmation of parity with evidence or (b) a concrete fix + tests.
- Fixtures updated to cover edge cases and avoid skipped paths.
- No SKIPPED branches remain in tests/conformance/crates/bubbles/mod.rs unless explicitly justified.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbles/src/*, tests/conformance/crates/bubbles/mod.rs, tests/conformance/fixtures/go_outputs/bubbles.json. Use legacy_bubbles as reference.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:54.737129295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:01:20.863512226Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:01:20.863491437Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete: stopwatch (6.1), timer (6.2), list (6.3), table (6.4), paginator (6.5), help (6.6), viewport (6.7), cursor (6.8), keybinding (6.9), filepicker (6.10), textinput (6.11), fixture consolidation (6.12). Bubbles conformance 83/83 (100%) with comprehensive unit test coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.6","depends_on_id":"bd-212m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:49:54.737129295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-212m.6.1","title":"Bubbles: stopwatch parity audit","description":"Audit Stopwatch component parity vs Go.

Scope:
- Compare Rust stopwatch behavior to legacy_bubbles stopwatch (start/stop/reset/tick).
- Expand fixtures to cover timing precision, reset semantics, and formatting.
- Add unit tests for tick progression and formatting output.
- Fix any discrepancies uncovered.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Conformance fixtures for stopwatch cover start/stop/reset and formatting edge cases.
- Unit tests validate elapsed time formatting and state transitions.
- Any discovered discrepancy is fixed and recorded in FEATURE_PARITY.md.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbles/src/stopwatch.rs, tests/conformance/crates/bubbles/mod.rs (stopwatch fixtures), tests/conformance/fixtures/go_outputs/bubbles.json.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:50:06.501803827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T16:37:35.219166711Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T16:37:35.218796624Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:50:06.501803827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-212m.9.1","title":"Glow conformance harness + fixtures","description":"Create Glow conformance harness and fixtures.

Scope:
- Implement Go reference capture at tests/conformance/go_reference/cmd/glow/main.go (if missing).
- Capture core behaviors: theme selection, width wrapping, pager output, stdin vs file input.
- Generate fixtures in tests/conformance/fixtures/go_outputs/glow.json.
- Implement Rust conformance tests comparing output.
- Add logging for diff outputs.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Glow conformance tests pass and are included in charmed_conformance.
- Fixtures cover core CLI options (theme, width, pager, stdin).
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated with Glow coverage status.","notes":"Files: tests/conformance/go_reference/cmd/glow, tests/conformance/crates/glow/mod.rs (new), crates/glow/src/main.rs.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:54:30.860704225Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:31:02.957528969Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:54:30.860704225Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Scope:
- Implement Wish conformance harness if missing.
- Ensure tests can run non-interactively (mock SSH session or harness).
- Gate with feature flags if needed but default to on once stable.
- Document any platform constraints.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:

- Tests are stable in CI/local without manual steps.

 {"id":"bd-212m.9.3","title":"Charmed-wasm conformance smoke tests","description":"Add conformance smoke tests for charmed-wasm (if applicable).

Scope:
- Identify wasm build targets or crates that should be validated.
- Create a minimal wasm test harness (e.g., render a styled string, ensure output).
- Run in CI or document manual steps using wasm-bindgen-test.
- Capture logs for failures.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- wasm smoke tests exist and run successfully in at least one automated environment.
- Tests validate basic rendering behavior and do not require manual interaction.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to mention wasm coverage.","notes":"Only proceed if wasm targets exist or are planned; otherwise document explicit exclusion.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:54:46.485328374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:31:18.061572576Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-212m.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-212m.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:54:46.485328374Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2q98","title":"Add bubbletea Println/Printf unmanaged output and rich error types","description":"Implement Bubbletea Println/Printf unmanaged output and richer error types to match Go ergonomics.

Scope:
- Add Program-level Println/Printf helpers that write outside the TUI renderer.
- Ensure output respects raw/alt-screen modes and restores terminal appropriately.
- Define richer error types for program failures (I/O, renderer, terminal).
- Add unit tests and integration tests for unmanaged output.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Println/Printf APIs documented and tested in both sync/async Program modes.
- Rich error types carry context and are surfaced to callers.
- Integration tests show unmanaged output without corrupting screen.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs, crates/bubbletea/src/error.rs (if present), tests in crates/bubbletea or tests/conformance.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:33.022679833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:32:26.620928032Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3o6n","title":"QA and Stabilization Session: wish, lipgloss, glamour, huh","description":"Completed a deep dive review and fix cycle for the codebase.

Fixed:
- wish: Output flushing, bubbletea integration, input handling.
- lipgloss: Theme initialization bugs, missing error types.
- glamour: Table rendering (replaced naive implementation with table.rs), updated tests.
- huh: Doc examples, missing helper methods for value extraction.
- charmed_log: Date math (migrated to chrono).

Verified:
- All crates compile cleanly.
- Tests pass for all modified crates.
- Workspace check passes.

Closed related epics/tasks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:22.301048189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525183754Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:46:29.525106669Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
